facts: Gears are wheels with teeth.
Gear teeth help wheels grip each other.
Two connected gears spin in opposite directions.
A small gear can make a big gear spin slower but with more power.
A big gear can make a small gear spin faster but with less power.
Gears can change speed, power, and direction.
Many machines use gears to move parts together.
Clock gears can move very slowly and very precisely.
Gears need oil or grease to work smoothly.
If gear teeth are damaged, the mechanism can jam.
Some gears can turn round motion into straight motion.
Gear systems are used in clocks, cars, machines, and toys.
A lever is a bar that turns around a fixed point.
The fixed point of a lever is called a fulcrum.
A lever can help lift heavy objects with less force.
A longer lever can give more lifting power.
A short lever can move faster but gives less power.
Levers can change the direction of force.
A seesaw is a simple example of a lever.
A crowbar uses lever power to move or lift objects.
Scissors use levers to make cutting easier.
A lever works best when the fulcrum is in the right place.
Many old machines used levers because they are simple and strong.
Levers are used in tools, traps, gates, engines, and game mechanisms.
A spring mechanism stores energy when a spring is squeezed or stretched.
A compressed spring pushes back when it is released.
A stretched spring pulls back toward its normal shape.
Springs can make movement smoother and less sudden.
A spring can return a part to its starting position.
Many toys use springs to create motion.
Pens often use small springs inside the click mechanism.
Some clocks use springs to store power.
A spring can absorb shocks and reduce vibration.
Different springs can be soft or stiff.
If a spring is too weak, it may not move the mechanism well.
Spring mechanisms are used in tools, locks, suspensions, and machines.
A linear actuator moves something in a straight line.
It changes energy into pushing or pulling motion.
Some linear actuators use electricity to move.
Some linear actuators use air or fluid pressure.
A linear actuator can open, close, lift, or slide parts.
It is useful when turning motion is not needed.
Many automatic doors use linear actuator movement.
Hospital beds can use linear actuators to change position.
Linear actuators can move very slowly and with control.
Some actuators are strong enough to lift heavy loads.
The travel distance of an actuator is called its stroke.
Linear actuators are used in robots, machines, vehicles, and home devices.
A piston is a part that moves back and forth inside a cylinder.
A piston can be pushed by gas, air, or liquid pressure.
When a piston moves, it can create force.
Pistons are important parts of many engines.
An engine piston helps turn fuel energy into motion.
A pump piston can push liquid through a system.
The tight fit between piston and cylinder helps control pressure.
Pistons often work in repeating cycles.
A piston can move in a straight line while other parts rotate.
Seals help stop pressure from leaking around a piston.
If a piston gets stuck, the mechanism may stop working.
Pistons are used in engines, pumps, compressors, and hydraulic systems.
A lock mechanism keeps a door, box, or device closed.
Many locks open only when the correct key or code is used.
Inside a lock, small parts must line up in the right way.
A key changes the positions of parts inside the lock.
Some locks use pins, while others use levers or discs.
A latch is the part that holds the lock closed.
Turning the key can move the latch and unlock the mechanism.
A good lock should be hard to open without permission.
Some locks are mechanical and need no electricity.
Other locks are electronic and can use cards, buttons, or sensors.
Locks need clean moving parts to work smoothly.
Lock mechanisms are used in doors, cabinets, bikes, safes, and machines.
A differential is a gear mechanism that lets connected wheels turn at different speeds.
It is very useful when a vehicle turns.
During a turn, the outer wheel travels farther than the inner wheel.
The differential helps both wheels keep moving smoothly in that situation.
Without a differential, turning can be harder and rougher.
A differential usually works together with axle shafts and gears.
It can send power to two wheels at the same time.
Small gears inside the differential help share movement.
Differentials are common in cars and other wheeled machines.
Some machines use special differentials to improve traction.
If a differential is damaged, the vehicle may make noise or turn badly.
A differential helps combine power transfer with flexible wheel motion.
